title: Sidekiq download credentials should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Sidekiq Pro/Enterprise download credentials detected in source code. These credentials provide access to download Sidekiq Pro and Enterprise gems from gems.contribsys.com, which are commercial products requiring a valid license. Exposing these credentials enables unauthorized download and use of paid Sidekiq gems by anyone with access to the repository. Attackers can use your credentials to download Sidekiq Pro/Enterprise gems without purchasing a license, violate your license agreement, redistribute the paid gems to others, potentially cause license termination by the vendor, and access premium features without authorization. Revoke these credentials immediately by contacting Sidekiq support, request new download credentials with a different username and password, store credentials securely using environment variables (BUNDLE_GEMS__CONTRIBSYS__COM), use bundle config for credential management instead of hardcoding in Gemfile, add Gemfile.lock and .bundle/config to .gitignore, implement secret scanning in CI/CD pipelines, and consider using credential helpers or secret management systems for sensitive gem source authentication.

Sidekiq download credentials detected in Bundler environment variable. The BUNDLE_GEMS__CONTRIBSYS__COM environment variable contains authentication credentials for accessing Sidekiq Pro/Enterprise gems. Exposing these credentials in source code, configuration files, or CI/CD pipelines allows unauthorized access to commercial Sidekiq gems without a valid license. Remove hardcoded credentials immediately, use secure environment variable management, implement proper secret storage solutions, and request new credentials from Sidekiq support if exposed.

Sidekiq download username and password detected in variable assignments. Sidekiq Pro/Enterprise require authentication credentials to download gems from the private gem server. These credentials are tied to your Sidekiq license and should never be committed to source code or stored in plaintext configuration files. Exposing these credentials enables unauthorized use of paid Sidekiq features and violates your license agreement. Remove credentials from code immediately, use environment variables or secret management systems, update your Sidekiq credentials through the customer portal, and implement pre-commit hooks to prevent future credential leaks.
